steps.title: Locate the defining contrast
steps.content: The opening Though and the phrase “novel path” establish a contrast. The researchers chose something novel (new, original), which tells us the many studies described first are not novel—they follow the standard, expected approach.

steps.title: Match the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Conventional directly means standard and traditional, expressing exactly what contrasts with the researchers' novel direction. The other three don't establish this contrast: eccentric suggests unusual practices (reversing the intended contrast), while meager and random address irrelevant factors.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
